params._note: Tunable knobs read by live_paper.py at startup + hot-reloaded each sweep. Written by the autonomous tuner (trading-platform/agents/tuner.py); every change is audited in market.db param_changes. Seeded 2026-06-22 to replicate the engine's hardcoded defaults exactly.

strategies.desc: Morning sector-dislocation fade (09:05–10:00), euro-neutral peer hedge — experiment size.
VWAP-reversion fade (10:00–B_END), market-neutral. Confirmed edge: morning z>3 10:00–11:30.

alerts.message: ORDER DIAG (5 fails): All failures are exit_unfilled marketable limits priced off a 15-min DELAYED `last`. The real market drifted past the stale limit during the delay window, so orders never crossed and cancelled after retries. Not symbol- or strategy-specific — it's the pricing source. | FIX: Stop pricing limits off delayed `last`; subscribe to real-time L1 and peg marketable limits to live bid/ask (or use MKT/MOC for exits). Widen cross only as fallback.

ORDER DIAG (18 fails): Exits are marketable limits priced off a 15-min DELAYED `last`, so limit prices lag the true book. In a moving market the limit sits behind the real cross, never fills across 3 retries despite 20–80bps allowance, then IBKR rejects with code 202 (auto-cancel). Not a venue/qty issue — stale pricing input. | FIX: Subscribe to real-time L1 (bid/ask) and price marketable exits off the live opposite-side quote, not delayed `last`; mark-out cross from live mid. Don't widen bps on
EXIT LEAK — a position could not be unwound; manual check needed
